expectations.text: The output searches or reasons about both Inbox and Sent Items, not just Inbox
expectations.evidence: Output states 'Searched the Outlook inbox for messages...' and the closing 'Note on scope' section confirms only a plain Inbox read was done; Sent Items is never mentioned.

expectations.text: The output groups results using the Email Triage Report section headers
expectations.evidence: Uses ad hoc headers ('Needs your attention', 'Retail / Ulta', 'Meetings', 'Asana notification noise', 'Amazon marketplace') with no relationship to the skill's URGENT/QUALITY/REGULATORY/PLM FLAGS structure.

expectations.text: Emails with signal for more than one sub-system are split and cross-referenced rather than filed under a single label
expectations.evidence: No sub-system labels (pd/ops/quality/regulatory/etc.) are applied anywhere in the output; items are grouped by informal topic only.

expectations.text: The output ends by asking which items to push rather than creating Asana tasks unprompted
expectations.evidence: Output ends with 'Say the word if you want the actual action items pulled out of any of them' — an offer to do more reading, not the push/skip confirmation gate the skill defines.

expectations.text: No Asana write happens before user confirmation
expectations.evidence: No write was attempted; explicitly stated 'nothing was written to Asana or PLM'. Note: this assertion is non-discriminating here — the eval run's own instructions forbade writes for both configurations, so this would pass regardless of skill quality (see eval_feedback).

suggestions.assertion: No Asana write happens before user confirmation
suggestions.reason: Both with-skill and baseline runs were instructed not to write, as a safety constraint for this eval batch. This assertion can't discriminate skill quality under that constraint — it will pass for any reasonably cautious agent. Worth replacing with something that tests the *content* of the proposed action instead.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline produces a genuinely useful, well-written inbox summary, but it's a different product entirely — no classification, no routing, no multi-system fan-out. This is the expected and correct gap: it's exactly what the skill's structured routing logic is supposed to add over a generic read.
